stories.title: GOP chair of House Judiciary Committee asks Justice Department to consider prosecuting Jack Smith
stories.summary: House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan has asked the Justice Department to consider criminally charging special counsel Jack Smith, alleging Smith made false statements during closed-door testimony last year. The referral is based on concerns over Smith's team obtaining text messages between Trump administration officials and Congress members. The Justice Department has confirmed receiving the referral and will investigate all evidence of criminal conduct. A decision on Smith's potential prosecution is now pending.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.trend: jim jordan jack smith doj referral

stories.title: Lil Wayne praises Trump's White House ballroom project, calls out critics who 'don't know nothing'
stories.summary: Rapper Lil Wayne praises President Trump's White House ballroom project, saying it will spare performers from being pushed outside for major events. The project aims to provide an indoor space for performances, events, and diplomatic ceremonies. Lil Wayne, who performed at a 2023 celebration marking the 50th anniversary of hip-hop, expressed support for the project after a memorable performance outside at Number One Observatory Circle. The ballroom is expected to accommodate guests and heightened security protocols.

stories.title: Tigers' Kenley Jansen: Extends scoreless streak Wednesday
stories.summary: Tigers' closer Kenley Jansen extended his scoreless streak to seven appearances, logging a clean inning of relief in Wednesday's 5-1 win over the Cubs. He walked one and struck out one. Jansen has now recorded a win and two saves during this streak, and leads Detroit with 11 saves this season. The Tigers are 1-1 in their series with the Cubs, with the final game scheduled for Thursday.

stories.title: Yordan continues MVP-caliber season with clutch go-ahead blast
stories.summary: Yordan Alvarez hit his Major League-leading 34th home run of the season, a two-run shot in the eighth inning, breaking a 2-2 tie and sending the Astros to a 5-2 win over the Marlins at Daikin Park. The Astros completed a three-game sweep of Miami on Wednesday night. Alvarez's 34 homers are three shy of his career high. The Astros' manager, Joe Espada, earned his 225th win with the team.

stories.title: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
stories.summary: US launches 12th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, killing at least two people, as Yemen's Houthis claim to have attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. The Houthis targeted the ships, Encelia and Layla, with drones and missiles, allegedly for violating their naval blockade. The attack would be the first since the Houthis announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia. The US has threatened to take action against the Houthis if they continue to block the Red Sea.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban is now in effect for over 20 million households in southern and eastern England and parts of Wales, restricting non-essential water use due to successive heatwaves straining water supplies. The ban affects Thames Water customers and prohibits activities like watering gardens and washing vehicles. This measure is a result of below-average spring and early summer rainfall, despite a wetter-than-usual winter. Water management and preparedness for drier summers expected with climate change are now under scrutiny.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a working joystick and button to control Mario's movement and jumping. The game includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The machine's design has reportedly won the approval of Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The set is now available for purchase.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A US government adviser claims China's Moonshot AI stole capabilities from top US AI model Anthropic's Fable AI through a process called distillation, gaining access to restricted Nvidia servers to train its K3 model. The alleged theft occurred through a weaker AI model extracting answers from a stronger one. This incident is linked to Moonshot's unveiling of its K3 model, which rivals top US technology. The US government is now investigating the claims.

stories.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
stories.summary: The Saudi authorities confirmed a strike on one of the ships. These are the first claimed attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's hottest regions are taking a toll on camels, with herders reporting blistered feet, watery eyes, and skin burns. In the Afar region of north-eastern Ethiopia, camel herder Ali Umer has lost eight camel calves in the past month due to extreme heat. The region is one of the driest places on Earth, where camels are often used to carry salt through the desert. As temperatures continue to rise, camel herders are facing unprecedented challenges.

stories.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
stories.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. The event will air live and as nightly highlights on channels like 7Plus in Australia and Channel 5 in the UK. North American viewers can watch the Games for free on CBC, Canada's national public broadcaster, using a virtual private network. The Games will be available to stream online from anywhere in the world.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal includes a bilateral safeguards agreement, providing great access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program. The agreement is expected to be a multi-billion-dollar partnership spanning decades. This move marks a significant step in advancing nuclear nonproliferation in the region.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy sector with a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods, set to take effect in August, citing the country's supply management system as an "unreasonable" barrier to American farmers. The system, which includes production quotas and set pricing, is a cornerstone of Canada's economy and rural communities. Canadian politicians, including Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette, have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable. The move is likely to escalate trade tensions between the two nations.

stories.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
stories.summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

stories.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
stories.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that up to 86% of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with most occurring during normal weather conditions, not heavy rain. The analysis, based on data from over 350 storm overflow outlets, found that none of the country's water companies performed well. This raises concerns about the legitimacy of sewage spills, which affect over 30 million people. The findings are set to be presented to Parliament, potentially leading to increased scrutiny of water companies' practices.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to help them walk independently. The drug, already available elsewhere in the UK, is being made available on the NHS in England. Clinical trials showed around four in 10 people taking the drug had improvements in their walking speed. This decision could significantly improve the lives of thousands of patients.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to growing spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. This comes as Alphabet's AI spending is expected to reach $205 billion this year, a significant increase from $190 billion. The company's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but its stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. Alphabet's AI spending is now a major focus, with $45 billion spent in the second quarter, mostly on servers and data centers.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks a collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who previously worked on "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." Yeon developed and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The ban is in addition to a three-game penalty already imposed by the Scottish FA. McInnes is appealing the decision, which he believes was wrong. The appeal will be a key test of the club's support for their manager.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo athlete, will share the honor. The flag will be returned to its owners after the ceremony, but they may have to compete for the first photo opportunity. The event marks a special moment for both athletes, who have been keeping the news secret for weeks.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A three-hour screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in 4DX format has left some viewers shaken and soaked. The 4DX experience, featuring seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology, is available at select theaters. Over 100 people have reported experiencing seasickness during the film's intense scenes. As a result, some theaters are considering adding a warning to the film's description.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The deal would come after Liverpool beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Talks around a new deal are also ongoing for Manchester United's Portugal midfielder Bruno Fernandes, 31. Fernandes has decided to commit his future to Manchester United.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report highlights a flaw in decades of British food policy, specifically the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which unfairly targets fat as a primary driver of weight gain. The committee recommends targeting ultra-processed foods high in sugar and refined carbohydrates instead. This shift could lead to more effective public health measures.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

The Justice Department has confirmed receiving the referral and will investigate all evidence of criminal conduct. A decision on Smith's potential prosecution is now pending.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
